title: pinus occidentalis var. maestraensis
intro: Pinus occidentalis var. maestraensis, also known as the Hispaniolan pine, is a variety of pine tree native to the island of Hispaniola, specifically found in the Dominican Republic and Haiti. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, derived from its resin and essential oils. The health benefits of Pinus occidentalis var. maestraensis include treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to aid in wound healing and reduce fever. Herbal preparations of this plant typically involve steaming the leaves and branches to release the essential oils, which can then be used in teas, poultices, or inhalations.
